title: China-to-Senegal EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: ASN Standards Route and IEC 61851 EVSE Baseline
rows.target_requirement: Senegalese EV charger projects should confirm whether ASN has adopted, referenced, or accepted an IEC-route standard for the charger scope, and whether the buyer, tender, customs route, or installer requires IEC 61851 evidence. IEC 61851-1:2017 covers EV supply equipment characteristics, EVSE-to-EV connection, and electrical safety for EV supply equipment up to 1 000 V AC or 1 500 V DC input limits stated by IEC.
target_standards: ASN Senegal national standards route where adopted or required
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly uses GB/T 18487.1 for conductive charging-system requirements together with GB/T 20234 connector standards and GB/T 27930 communication for DC charging. These documents can support a Senegal technical file only after clause mapping, edition comparison, and export-configuration checks against IEC 61851 and any ASN, tender, or owner requirement.
rows.gap: Do not state that a China GB/T test report is equivalent to Senegal acceptance unless the exact charger variant, protection functions, EMC evidence, connector interface, language/marking, and installation assumptions are mapped to the target route and accepted by the Senegal buyer or authority.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where Senegal law, ASN adoption, customs conformity route, tender, buyer specification, utility/site approval, or project-owner documents require IEC 61851 or an ASN-adopted equivalent
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T evidence is mandatory or expected for China-market conformity depending on the product route; it is supporting evidence only for Senegal unless accepted by the target route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat IEC 61851 as the target technical baseline only when ASN, a tender, customs route, buyer, Senelec, or project owner makes it applicable. GB/T 18487 evidence should be mapped, not renamed.

rows.requirement: China GB/T 18487 Evidence vs Senegal Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Senegal acceptance should be based on the current ASN, IEC-route, customs, CRSE/Senelec, tender, and project-owner requirements for the specific charger model and site. China GB/T 18487 evidence can support technical due diligence only where mapped to the target requirement and supported by the actual export configuration.
rows.china_equivalent: GB/T 18487.1-2023 is China's common conductive charging-system baseline and is used with GB/T 20234 connector standards and GB/T 27930 DC communication. It is not automatically equivalent to an IEC 61851 / IEC 62196 export configuration because connector hardware, communication behavior, protection design, labeling, and test edition may differ.
rows.gap: Build a cross-standard matrix before quoting: GB/T clause, IEC or Senegal target clause, hardware variant, connector variant, software region setting, test evidence, label/manual change, residual risk, and owner or authority confirmation. Limit equivalence claims to tested and mapped functions.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only to the extent Senegal law, ASN adoption, customs conformity, CRSE/Senelec approval, tender, or owner documents require the mapped item
mandatory_status.cn: China-market GB/T evidence is mandatory or expected for domestic conformity depending on the product and certification route; it is not a Senegal market approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 18487 is useful evidence, not a passport. For Senegal, convert China reports into a mapped IEC/ASN/local compliance file and confirm acceptance with the importer, project owner, CRSE/Senelec route, and site process.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation / Standardization Administration of China National Public Service Platform for Standards Information

rows.target_requirement: Where a Senegal project specifies IEC connector interfaces, AC and DC charger hardware should be checked against IEC 62196. IEC 62196-2:2025 covers AC pin-and-contact-tube accessories, while IEC 62196-3:2022 covers DC and AC/DC vehicle couplers; for DC fast charging, specify CCS2 / Combo 2 under IEC 62196-3 when that is the fleet or tender interface. Connector choice can also be driven by the imported vehicle fleet, tender requirements, site-owner requirements, and interoperability expectations.
target_standards: CCS2 / Combo 2 under IEC 62196-3 for DC fast charging where specified
Project fleet and tender connector specification
rows.china_equivalent: China-market EV chargers commonly use GB/T 20234.2 for AC connectors and GB/T 20234.3 for DC connectors, with GB/T 20234.1 general requirements. GB/T connector geometry, pin gender, signaling, and DC communication assumptions can differ from IEC 62196 Type 2 / CCS-style export configurations, so China hardware is not automatically plug-compatible.
rows.gap: Confirm connector drawings, vehicle inlet assumptions, cable ratings, locking, touch protection, pilot/proximity circuits, DC communication route, label language, and spare-part strategy before shipment. A connector adapter is not a compliance substitute unless the target route explicitly accepts it.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the Senegal project, tender, fleet, ASN-adopted standard, customs route, or site owner requires IEC 62196 or a specified connector interface; otherwise project-specific
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 20234 connector evidence is relevant for China-market configuration but does not prove IEC 62196 plug compatibility

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Connector mismatch is a high-risk China-to-Senegal gap. Verify the vehicle fleet and IEC 62196 route before promising GB/T hardware compatibility.

rows.target_requirement: Senegal charger configurations should be validated for 50 Hz operation and the actual site supply, commonly including 230 V single-phase or 400 V three-phase design assumptions where confirmed by the utility or project engineer. AC chargers, DC charger input stages, transformers, meters, cooling systems, auxiliary power supplies, and protection relays should be checked against Senelec service conditions before shipment and commissioning.
target_standards: Senelec service conditions and site supply / metering requirements as the local grid reference
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic EV chargers are also commonly designed for 50 Hz operation, which reduces but does not eliminate export risk. Site voltage, phase configuration, earthing, neutral arrangement, surge protection, RCD/RCM behavior, EMC, labels, and software region settings still need Senegal-specific confirmation.
rows.gap: Provide Senegal configuration sheets showing 50 Hz ratings, input voltage tolerance, phase configuration, derating rules, transformer compatibility, protection settings, RCD/RCM design, cooling-system ratings, and commissioning tests. Confirm that China-only labels and software defaults are removed.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as an engineering, safety, and utility-connection requirement where Senegal site supply and Senelec service conditions apply
mandatory_status.cn: China 50 Hz evidence is relevant but not sufficient without Senegal site-voltage and installation confirmation

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Frequency alone is not the main China-to-Senegal gap because both markets commonly use 50 Hz. The export file still needs Senegal site voltage, protection, earthing, labeling, and commissioning evidence.

rows.requirement: Senegal EV Policy Context vs Charger Approval
rows.target_requirement: Senegal climate, energy-transition, transport, and electrification policy can support EV charging demand, and MITTA announced a Regulatory Framework for Electric Mobility in July 2024 as EV policy context. Policy support should not be treated as a charger product approval, tax ruling, public-charging operating authorization, customs clearance decision, or Senelec connection approval. EV charger obligations remain tied to the concrete product, site, import route, tender, tariff, and operator model.
target_standards: Senegal Nationally Determined Contribution transport and energy policy context
MITTA Regulatory Framework for Electric Mobility, July 2024
Senegal electricity-sector policy and CRSE-regulated operating context
Project tender or concession documents where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China policy support for new-energy vehicles and charging infrastructure is separate from technical conformity under GB/T 18487, GB/T 20234, and GB/T 27930. Domestic Chinese policy or subsidy eligibility does not establish Senegal product acceptance or a right to operate public charging.
china_standards: China new-energy vehicle and charging-infrastructure policy context
rows.gap: Keep the policy memo separate from the technical compliance file. For each Senegal project, identify whether public operation, billing, land use, electricity resale, tax incentives, import conformity, grid connection, or procurement rules create binding requirements beyond charger hardware testing.

mandatory_status.target: Informational for product compliance unless a Senegal law, tax measure, tender, concession, CRSE/Senelec process, or public-charging authorization makes a requirement binding
mandatory_status.cn: Informational for export approval; Chinese policy support does not replace China technical conformity or Senegal acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EV policy is market context, not charger approval. Document the separate Senegal technical, import, grid, tariff, and operating approvals for each site.

source.publisher: Ministere des Infrastructures, des Transports Terrestres et Aeriens (MITTA), Senegal

rows.requirement: Senelec Grid Connection and CRSE Electricity-Sector Context
rows.target_requirement: Senegal charger sites should treat Senelec distribution connection, metering, protection, available capacity, earthing, civil works, tariff, and commissioning conditions as site approvals separate from product testing. CRSE regulates the electricity-sector framework, while Senelec is the practical utility counterparty for many grid-connection and service conditions.
target_standards: Project-specific distribution capacity and metering approval
rows.china_equivalent: China charger files may include domestic grid-connection assumptions, GB/T 18487 protection functions, and site commissioning reports. These can support engineering review but do not replace Senelec capacity confirmation, metering arrangement, protection coordination, tariff treatment, or local commissioning acceptance.
rows.gap: Prepare a Senegal site pack with single-line diagram, load study, transformer/cable ratings, harmonics and power-quality assumptions, earthing design, protection settings, metering layout, emergency isolation, and commissioning method statement for Senelec, owner, and installer review.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for any grid-connected site to the extent Senelec service, CRSE-regulated electricity rules, local permits, tariff conditions, or project-owner approvals apply
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic grid assumptions are informational for export and must be replaced by Senegal site-specific utility approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Product conformity does not grant a Senelec connection. Treat grid connection, metering, protection, and commissioning as a separate Senegal site-approval workstream.

source.publisher: Commission de Regulation du Secteur de l'Energie (CRSE), Senegal
